original_hypothesis.title: Owner Can Drain User-Allocated TITN via Unrestricted Withdraw
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The withdraw function in MergeTgt.sol allows the owner to transfer any amount of any ERC20 token out of the contract, including the TITN tokens backing users' claimable balances. This breaks the solvency invariant and allows a rug pull.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Privileged functions that can arbitrarily drain user funds violate core trust assumptions, even if the owner is currently trusted.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Unrestricted admin withdrawal of protocol assets

preconditions: Owner account is malicious or compromised

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Owner can withdraw TITN needed for user claims

analysis_questions: Does the withdraw function bypass all accounting logic?
What happens to user claims if the balance is drained?

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: A compromised or malicious owner calls withdraw(titn, balance). The contract is drained. Users attempting to call claimTitn or withdrawRemainingTitn experience transaction reverts due to insufficient balance or underflow.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Total loss of all user-allocated TITN

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing tests ensuring owner cannot withdraw below totalTitnClaimable

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Observable via large owner withdrawal events

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
